concept: Grind / Reduce to Meal / Break Small Between Surfaces
description: To grind: grain to flour, herbs to powder, the year's harvest to the winter's bread. Reduction between stone and stone — the kitchen's oldest transformation. Kin to 'kuma' (drum) only in sound; kin to 'pharelo' (cook) in purpose.
sound_symbolism: 'ku' is the firm stone, 're' the turning repetition, 'mi' the fineness it all comes down to. The quern turns inside the word.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'lo mia lila nuora kuremi.' (We grind for food.) With stones: 'shia roe kerou kuremi.' (They grind with stones.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: The hand-mill and the water-mill — grinding is the work that renewable power learned first and never forgot.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The quern-stone's hours were the price of every loaf; grinding was the labor that made agriculture edible.
